assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states that Google Cloud can preempt Spot VMs at any time and explains how this interacts with the 25-second warning during the upgrade drain process.

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: The model warns to 'ensure' workloads are fault-tolerant as a mitigation, but does not point out that they likely already are or frame this as an existing benefit for upgrades.

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: The model actually recommends keeping maxSurge low (e.g., 1 or 2) to request VMs in small batches to avoid capacity limits.

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: The model does not mention the maxUnavailable setting at all.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: The model clearly explains that lack of Spot capacity in chosen regions/zones will prevent GCP from provisioning surge nodes, stalling the upgrade.

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: The model discusses PDBs but focuses on the risk of them stalling the upgrade and causing forceful termination after 1 hour; it does not explicitly recommend adding them for orderly drains.

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: The model does not provide any gcloud commands in its response.

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: The model explicitly suggests the opposite, stating 'Upgrade your On-Demand pools first'.
